markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eye Slash](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4740)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 120

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Small scars or marks around your eye improve your distant vision. These scars are especially common among [orc](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=66) scouts, who favor scars shaped like eagle talons. You can see four times farther than normal. If you have [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), you can see blood in color. Higher-level versions of an eye slash are larger and more elaborate scars or marks, radiating out around the eye.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Eye Slash
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 120

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Eye Slash (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 120

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The tattoo also grants you a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks that involve sight.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Eye Slash (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 120

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The tattoo also grants you a +2 item bonus to Perception checks that involve sight.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Eye Slash (True)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 120

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The tattoo also grants you a +3 item bonus to Perception checks that involve sight
name: Eye Slash (Major)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Faith Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4741)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 120

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You have marked your body to show your devotion to a deity. This tattoo could be the deity’s religious symbol, another image that evokes that deity, or another mark you gained through your devotion. The tattoo serves as a silver [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745) of the deity. Provided you keep the tattoo uncovered, you need not wield it to gain that benefit.

If you cease meeting the prerequisites, the tattoo fades, and you lose its benefits until you perform an [atone](/Rituals.aspx?ID=108) ritual and meet the prerequisites thereafter.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), or the 1st-level spell from your deity’s cleric spells. You can choose harm or heal only in accord with the deity’s divine font. If the deity allows either spell, choose one the tattoo can cast when you receive the tattoo. The DC for any of these spells is 18. Also, the tattoo has as a trait the school of the spell the tattoo can cast.

**Craft Requirements** silver worth 2 gp to include in the ink

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Faith Tattoo
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 120

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Faith Tattoo (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 120

**Price** 425 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The spell is 3rd level, and its DC is 24.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Faith Tattoo (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 120

**Price** 1,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The spell is 5th level, and its DC is 30.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Faith Tattoo (True)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 120

**Price** 8,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The spell is 7th level, and its DC is 35.
name: Faith Tattoo

    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Frostwalker Pattern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4742)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

</row>

</row>

</column>

---

Northern peoples design magical tattoos to protect against wintry weather, typically in geometric patterns with a combination of straight lines and whorls. This tattoo negates any damage you take from [severe environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) and reduces damage you take from extreme cold to equal that of severe cold.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Until the end of your next turn, you [ignore difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) from ice and snow and don’t risk falling when crossing ice.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Frostwalker Pattern

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Homeward Swallow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4743)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 120

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small tattoo of a swallow always points toward your home. The tattooing must take place at a location you consider to be your home, or the magic fails to bind with the ink. When you travel to your home using [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) that can be off target, such as [_teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720) or [_plane shift_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576), you arrive exactly at your home. If your home is destroyed or you come to believe a new place is your home, this tattoo fades from your skin.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You sense the direction toward your home.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Homeward Swallow

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Memoir Map](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4744)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 120

**Price** 13 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your journeys and the major events in your life that occur after you obtain this tattoo appear on it, your life story traced upon your skin. Each time you journey somewhere new or accomplish something noteworthy to you, a design or symbol appears, representing the event. The positions of these images are relative in location, but measurements aren’t exact. A memoir map starts with an icon representing your location when you receive the tattoo, usually over the heart, and grows from there. Traveling to another plane causes a new portion to appear on a different part of your body to represent that plane. If you want a record of your life before you receive your memoir map, you can have the tattoo artist embellish the map to represent past events.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Memoir Map

    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mortal Chronicle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4745)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 121

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Common among fatalists and adventurers with access to resurrection magic, mortal chronicles look like tombstones, funeral plaques, or simple scrolls bearing the wearer’s name or nickname. If you die, the date and cause of your death appear on the tattoo. The cause is literal and inexact, failing to identify specifics; it could read “beheaded” or “immolated” but not “beheaded by Amiri” or “murdered with fire.” If you’re raised from the dead, a mark on the tattoo indicates the date you reversed your death. The tattoo then expands enough to list your next death when it comes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mortal Chronicle

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Nemesis Name](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4747)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 121

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You want revenge badly enough that you tattoo your nemesis’s name in a place you can easily see to remember your vow to settle the score. Anyone who sees the tattoo senses your hatred of the named being. Receiving a new nemesis name makes any you already have non-magical. 

You gain a +2 status bonus to damage rolls against the creature named on your tattoo, but this nemesis gains a +1 status bonus to damage rolls against you.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per round;  **Requirements** You can see your nemesis, and they’re within 30 feet of you; **Effect** You focus your hatred into a mental scream. Your nemesis takes 3d6 mental damage, which they can resist with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) DC 26 Will save. You take half as much damage as your nemesis does, and you can’t reduce this damage in any way.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Nemesis Name

[One Hundred Victories](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4748)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

One hundred cuts, healed into diamond-shaped scars, represent the ability to withstand the attacks of your enemies. [Orc](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=66) warriors covet these scar patterns and cluster them around what they consider to be their strongest assets—a pattern around the heart signifies a warrior with great endurance, while one along the arms indicates great upper body strength. 

You gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks. If you have Ferocity (such as from the Orc Ferocity feat or a similar ability), the first time each day you use it, instead of remaining at 1 Hit Point, your Hit Points are set to an amount equal to your ancestry Hit Points, even if this amount is more than you had before using [Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=63).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: One Hundred Victories

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

</row>

</column>

---

These tattoos on your knuckles look like strange glyphs in an unknown language. If you press your fingertips to text in any language, these glyphs cycle in appearance through those of various extant languages. Encrypted text causes your glyphs to turn to a recognizable “null” symbol.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You sync the tattoos with the text your fingertips are touching. By running your fingers across the text, you translate it, with glyphs on your knuckles showing the translation in a language you can read. Your tattooed glyphs can’t translate encrypted or encoded text, language couched in metaphor, and the like, subject to GM discretion.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Reading Glyphs

Wings, normally tattooed on the upper back, enable you to fly when activated. The visual manifestation is typically a slight glow or ripple in the ink, but some artists make it so the tattoo creates a glowing aura or lines of light in the shape of wings.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For 10 minutes, you gain a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) equal to either your land Speed or 20 feet, whichever is greater.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Staring Skull](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4751)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122

**Price** 550 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo usually depicts a humanoid skull with staring eyes in its sockets, but any creature with two eyes and a skull is possible, such as a [wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3241), [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3137), or [psychopomp](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=458). When your [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition increases to a value that would kill you, this tattoo reduces your dying value to 1 fewer than would kill you. If a [death effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331) would kill you—provided the effect is from a creature of 8th level or lower or a spell of 4th level or lower—this tattoo activates and keeps you alive instead. You can benefit from this ability only once per day. Each time the tattoo prevents you from dying, one of its eyes disappears, the image now featuring either an empty socket or an eyepatch or other covering. After both eyes vanish, the tattoo becomes non-magical and no longer protects you. If you receive a new _staring skull_ tattoo, any other you have loses its staring eyes and becomes non-magical.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Staring Skull

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sun Sight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4752)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122

**Price** 850 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Placed under the eyes like rays of light, this tattoo burns away illusions with the unmerciful brilliance of the sun. You gain a +2 bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks that involve sight. If you’re [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65), you receive a new save at the start of each of your turns to end your dazzled condition.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts a 4th-level [_scorching ray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450), with the rays emitting from your eyes. The number of actions you spend Activating the tattoo determines scorching ray’s number of rays. The tattoo also attempts to dispel each illusion on a creature hit by a ray ([counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) level 5th, counteract modifier +19).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sun Sight

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tooth and Claw Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4753)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo resembles an animal’s fangs, claws, or similar natural weapons, letting you wield such weapons and turn into the same beast. When you receive the tattoo, choose the animal from among the following: ape, bear, bull, canine, cat, deer, frog, shark, or snake. You can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) skill with animals of that kind. This tattoo is usually located on the body part or parts it’s meant to transform—on the back of the hands for claws, around the mouth for jaws, on the forehead for horns, and so on..

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command ([morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=657))    **Effect** You gain an unarmed attack matching the tattoo for 1 minute. It has the same damage as your best unarmed attack and has the same traits. Its damage type is bludgeoning for a fist or frog’s jaws; piercing for an antler, fangs, horns, or most jaws; or slashing for claws..

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision ([polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670))    **Effect** The tattoo casts 3rd-level [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1440) to transform you into the animal that matches your tattoo.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tooth and Claw Tattoo
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tooth and Claw Tattoo (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The tattoo casts 4th-level _animal form_ instead of 3rd.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tooth and Claw Tattoo (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The tattoo casts 5th-level _animal form_ instead of 3rd.
name: Tooth and Claw Tattoo

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tradecraft Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4754)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Crafters choose tattoos that represent their dedication and skill in their chosen field. Such tattoos might adorn the arm, fingers, or eyes, and they take the form of artistic patterns or depict tools of the trade, such as anvils, paintbrushes, or trowels. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. Furthermore, when you roll a critical failure on a Crafting check to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=2364), treat it as a failure instead.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts [_creation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1477). You choose the item and its appearance, and whether the spell is 4th or 5th level.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tradecraft Tattoo
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tradecraft Tattoo (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122

**Price** 13,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks is +3. The creation spell’s duration is unlimited but ends when you Activate the tattoo again.
name: Tradecraft Tattoo

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Triangular Teeth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4755)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122

**Price** 33 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Rows of triangles symbolizing shark teeth protect you from danger and enable you to take fierce retaliation against those who try to harm you. Seafarers, especially those on the seas of Minata, wear these tattoos in patterns, with multiple rows of regular triangles. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to navigate bodies of water.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would be hit by an attack against your AC;  **Effect** You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against the attack, or a +2 circumstance bonus if the attacker is in water or has the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=529), [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=533), or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) trait. Whether the attack hits or misses, you gain a +2 status bonus to damage with the next [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) you make against the attacker before the end of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Triangular Teeth

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122 **Price** 60 gp **Usage** tattooed on the body --- The name of your beloved adorns this stylized image of a heart. An unbreakable heart tattoo can be received only at the end of a successful [_heartbond_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=149) ritual, serving as your token. The other participant can have a token other than a tattoo if they wish. If you have more than one _heartbond_, each unbreakable heart you have serves as a token for only one of them. The love exuding from you grants you a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks. When you use heartbond’s activity to learn your beloved’s present state, you can also grant them a small gift, choosing from the following options each time. Make your choice after learning their state.
  • Your beloved gains 5 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 10 minutes.
  • The tattoo casts [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549) on your beloved.
  • The tattoo casts [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689) on your beloved.
  • The tattoo casts [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) on your beloved’s token from _heartbond_.
name: Unbreakable Heart

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Varisian Emblem](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4757)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These tattoos are created using designs and techniques from Varisian culture. Such tattoos often depict the Varisian rune for one of the seven schools of magic, but one might instead show a creature or celestial phenomenon associated with the school, depicted in traditional Varisian iconography. The tattoo allows you to understand and speak Varisian. If you already know that language, you instead gain a +1 item bonus on [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks you make when speaking [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=174) to someone who understands it.

Each description lists the school of each Varisian emblem, and the tattoo has the trait of that school. The spell the tattoo can cast is from its school. As with the Thassilonian magic that was once so prevalent in Varisia, these tattoos lack a symbol of [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47).

**Activate** <actions string="" /> The actions required to Activate the tattoo are the same as those needed to cast its spell;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts its spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These tattoos are created using designs and techniques from Varisian culture. Such tattoos often depict the Varisian rune for one of the seven schools of magic, but one might instead show a creature or celestial phenomenon associated with the school, depicted in traditional Varisian iconography. The tattoo allows you to understand and speak Varisian. If you already know that language, you instead gain a +1 item bonus on [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks you make when speaking [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=174) to someone who understands it.

Each description lists the school of each Varisian emblem, and the tattoo has the trait of that school. The spell the tattoo can cast is from its school. As with the Thassilonian magic that was once so prevalent in Varisia, these tattoos lack a symbol of [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47).

**Activate** <actions string="" /> The actions required to Activate the tattoo are the same as those needed to cast its spell;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts its spell.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Varisian Emblem](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4757)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These tattoos are created using designs and techniques from Varisian culture. Such tattoos often depict the Varisian rune for one of the seven schools of magic, but one might instead show a creature or celestial phenomenon associated with the school, depicted in traditional Varisian iconography. The tattoo allows you to understand and speak Varisian. If you already know that language, you instead gain a +1 item bonus on [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks you make when speaking [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=174) to someone who understands it.

Each description lists the school of each Varisian emblem, and the tattoo has the trait of that school. The spell the tattoo can cast is from its school. As with the Thassilonian magic that was once so prevalent in Varisia, these tattoos lack a symbol of [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47).

**Activate** <actions string="" /> The actions required to Activate the tattoo are the same as those needed to cast its spell;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts its spell.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wildwood Ink](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4758)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These curving, delicate designs resemble leaves, vines, or creepers, most often wrapped around a limb, ear, or throat, or curled around specific muscles. They help you blend in among plants. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks, which increases to +2 in forests.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature would detect you by Seeking; **Requirements** You’re in a forest or similar natural area; **Effect** The tattoo casts tree shape on you before you can be noticed. The duration of this spell is 10 minutes.

If you’ve already Activated the tattoo, you can supply a separate casting of [_tree shape_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) to recharge the tattoo instead of having the spell’s normal effect. This allows you to Activate the tattoo again in the same day. You can do so multiple times each day, but only as many times as you continue to cast tree shape to recharge the tattoo after each use.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wildwood Ink
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wildwood Ink (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to Stealth checks is +2, or +3 in forests. Tree shape lasts up to 1 hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wildwood Ink (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to Stealth checks is +3, or +4 in forests. Tree shape lasts up to 8 hours.
name: Wildwood Ink

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123 **Usage** tattooed on the body --- Depicting a majestic dragon in flight, this tattoo imparts the endurance and majesty of those powerful creatures. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to the damage type matching the dragon your tattoo depicts.
  • **Black or Copper** [ Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524)
  • **Blue or Bronze** [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)
  • **Brass, Gold, or Red** [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)
  • **Green** [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)
  • **Silver or White** [ Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555)
**Activate** command, envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The tattoo casts [_dragon wings_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2104) on you. You can add the dragon claws benefit only if you know that focus spell and spend 1 Focus Point when you Activate the tattoo. Wyrm on the Wing **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123 **Price** 700 gp Wyrm on the Wing (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123 **Price** 2,800 gp --- The resistance increases to 10. The tattoo gains the following activation. **Activate** command, envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The tattoo casts [_dragon form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1502) on you, turning you into the type of dragon represented by the tattoo. Wyrm on the Wing (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123 **Price** 13,500 gp --- The resistance increases to 15. The tattoo gains the activation of the greater type, but the _dragon form_ is heightened to 8th level.
name: Wyrm on the Wing

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 124 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- This silver disc displays an ever-changing etching of an animal. When you affix the _beastmaster’s sigil_, the animal settles into a single form based on where you affix it, showing the animal the item can summon when affixed that way. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 19. A weapon can benefit from only the melee weapon benefit or ranged weapon benefit. A thrown melee weapon gets the melee weapon benefit, and a combination weapon gets only one benefit, which you choose when you affix the spellheart. If it’s unclear which one should apply, the GM decides.
  • **Armor** The sigil grants you a +1 item bonus to saving throws against poison.
  • **Melee Weapo**n The sigil grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382).
  • **Ranged Weapon** If you critically hit with the affixed weapon, and the target is adjacent to a creature summoned with the sigil, the target takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_tame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1014). Beastmaster's Sigil **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 124 **Price** 160 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 3rd-level _summon animal_ to summon a [giant monitor lizard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3088) (armor), [wolverine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=862) (melee), or [giant bat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2849) (ranged). Beastmaster's Sigil (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 124 **Price** 360 gp --- The spell DC is 23 and the bleed damage is 2d6. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-level _summon animal_ to summon a [giant scorpion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3175) (armor), [dire wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3242) (melee), or [fen mosquito swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=732) (ranged). Beastmaster's Sigil (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 700 gp --- The spell DC is 25, the item bonuses are +2, and the bleed damage is 3d6. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_tanglefoot_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713).
name: Beastmaster's Sigil

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- Appearing as vibrant as the day they were picked, this cluster of flowers is arranged in a small spray, tied with a red satin ribbon. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 24.
  • **Armor** You gain the ability to speak with flowers, as [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681).
  • **Weapon** ([disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) After you cast a plant spell by activating the posy, pollen coats your weapon. Your next Strike causes the target to be [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 on a hit (sickened 2 on a critical hit). If the creature attempts to recover, it sneezes rather than retching (rolling against the spellheart’s spell DC). If you don’t make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) by the end of your next turn, the pollen becomes inert. Plant creatures are immune.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_tanglefoot_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713). Brightbloom Posy **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 450 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_soothing blossoms_](/Spells.aspx?ID=999). Brightbloom Posy (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 1,300 gp --- The spell DC is 28. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_soothing blossoms_](/Spells.aspx?ID=999). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_petal storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=967). Brightbloom Posy (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 36,000 gp --- The spell DC is 41. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 6th-level [_petal storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=967). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_burning blossoms_](/Spells.aspx?ID=878).
name: Brightbloom Posy

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- Appearing as vibrant as the day they were picked, this cluster of flowers is arranged in a small spray, tied with a red satin ribbon. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 24.
  • **Armor** You gain the ability to speak with flowers, as [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681).
  • **Weapon** ([disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) After you cast a plant spell by activating the posy, pollen coats your weapon. Your next Strike causes the target to be [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 on a hit (sickened 2 on a critical hit). If the creature attempts to recover, it sneezes rather than retching (rolling against the spellheart’s spell DC). If you don’t make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) by the end of your next turn, the pollen becomes inert. Plant creatures are immune.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_tanglefoot_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713). markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- The surface of this golden, heart-shaped locket is nearly worn through with cracks. If opened, it reveals a portrait of someone the bearer loved dearly and has lost. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 24.
  • **Armor** The desolation locket numbs you to further despair, and you gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against emotion effects.
  • **Weapon** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) After you cast an [enchantment](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=4) spell by activating the locket, you emanate an aura of hopelessness in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) until the start of your next turn. A creature that ends its turn in the aura must succeed at a Will saving throw against the locket’s spell DC or become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (slowed 2 on a critical failure) until the end of its next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_haunting hymn_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1999). Desolation Locket **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 470 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_agonizing despair_](/Spells.aspx?ID=665). Desolation Locket (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 2,800 gp --- The item bonus when affixed to armor is +3, and the spell DC is 30. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-level [_agonizing despair_](/Spells.aspx?ID=665). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_crushing despair_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757). Desolation Locket (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 34,000 gp --- The item bonus when affixed to armor is +4; when you critically fail a saving throw against an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effect, you get a failure instead; and the spell DC is 41. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 7th-level _crushing despair_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_canticle of everlasting grief_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1459).
name: Desolation Locket

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- Mist fills the glass of this small circular hand mirror, creating strange patterns in the shifting gray wisps. The back of the mirror bears a flowing script engraving in an unknown language. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 23.
  • **Armor** After you cast an [illusion](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=6) spell by activating the mirror, choose one ally within 30 feet. That ally gains a single illusory image, per [_mirror image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=197). The image lasts until the end of your next turn if not destroyed before then.
  • **Weapon** After you cast an illusion spell by activating the mirror, illusory copies of the weapon swirl in the air around it. You’re affected by the [_thicket of knives_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1017) spell until the end of your next turn. The spell ends if you stop wielding the affixed weapon.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535). Enigma Mirror **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 325 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_mirror image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=197). Enigma Mirror (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 950 gp --- The spell DC is 27. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_mirror’s misfortune_](/Spells.aspx?ID=951). Enigma Mirror (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 2,750 gp --- The spell DC is 30. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_mirror’s misfortune_](/Spells.aspx?ID=951). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_mirror malefactors_](/Spells.aspx?ID=950).
name: Enigma Mirror

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- This miniature wooden planchette is engraved with symbols designed to attract spirits. When affixed, the symbols begin to glow, and the planchette turns slightly insubstantial. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 29.
  • **Armor** After you cast a [necromancy](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=7) spell by activating the planchette, you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to all physical damage (except [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840)) until the start of your next turn. Attacks by incorporeal creatures (such as a ghost’s ghostly hand) count as ghost touch.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a necromancy spell by activating the planchette, the affixed weapon gains the effects of the _ghost touch_ property rune until the end of your next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_chill touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745). Ghostcaller's Planchette **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 1,750 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_invoke spirits_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578). Ghostcaller's Planchette (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 21,000 gp --- The resistance when affixed to armor is 10. When the planchette is affixed to a weapon, casting a necromancy spell by activating the planchette grants the weapon the effects of both the _ghost touch_ and [_greater disrupting_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2852) property runes. The spell DC is 38. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 7th-level _invoke spirits_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_tempest of shades_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1015).
name: Ghostcaller's Planchette

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- Contained within this small glass tube is a twisted wire filament crackling with electricity, sending static prickling through the hair of anyone holding it. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 17.
  • **Armor** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 2 to electricity.
  • **Weapon** After you cast an [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) spell by activating the coil, your Strikes with the weapon deal an additional 1d4 electricity damage until the end of your next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_electric arc_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509). Jolt Coil **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Price** 60 gp Jolt Coil (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Price** 475 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 3, extra damage when affixed to a weapon is 1d6, and the spell DC is 24. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586). Jolt Coil (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Price** 1,750 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 5, extra damage when affixed to a weapon is 1d8, and the spell DC is 29. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_mirror image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=197). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_draw the lightning_](/Spells.aspx?ID=898).
name: Jolt Coil

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- The golden religious symbol on the top of this spherical incense holder shifts its form to match the faith of its bearer. You gain no benefit from a judgment thurible if you don't worship a deity or if your deity is true neutral. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 27.
  • **Armor** After you cast a spell by activating the thurible, the thurible casts [_circle of protection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=38) on you, with a duration of 1 round. You choose the alignment, which must be one opposed by one of your deity's alignments.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a spell by activating the thurible, the weapon gains your choice of an _[anarchic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=290), [axiomatic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=291), [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2842)_, or _[unholy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2851)_ rune until the end of your next turn. You can choose a rune only if it matches an alignment your deity has (_anarchic_ for _chaotic_, _axiomatic_ for lawful, _holy_ for good, or _unholy_ for evil).
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_divine lance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498). Judgment Thurible **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Price** 975 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499). Judgment Thurible (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Price** 14,000 gp --- The additional damage from the rune granted by the thurible is 2d6, and the spell DC is 35. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 6th-level _divine wrath._ **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_divine decree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495). Judgment Thurible (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Price** 65,000 gp --- The additional damage from the rune granted by the thurible is 2d6, and the spell DC is 43. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 7th-level _divine decree_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_summon deific herald_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1007).
name: Judgement Thurible

summary_markdown: The additional damage from the rune granted by the thurible is 2d6, and the spell DC is 35.

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- The golden religious symbol on the top of this spherical incense holder shifts its form to match the faith of its bearer. You gain no benefit from a judgment thurible if you don't worship a deity or if your deity is true neutral. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 27.
  • **Armor** After you cast a spell by activating the thurible, the thurible casts [_circle of protection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=38) on you, with a duration of 1 round. You choose the alignment, which must be one opposed by one of your deity's alignments.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a spell by activating the thurible, the weapon gains your choice of an _[anarchic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=290), [axiomatic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=291), [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2842)_, or _[unholy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2851)_ rune until the end of your next turn. You can choose a rune only if it matches an alignment your deity has (_anarchic_ for _chaotic_, _axiomatic_ for lawful, _holy_ for good, or _unholy_ for evil).
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_divine lance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498). Judgment Thurible **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Price** 975 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499). Judgment Thurible (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Price** 14,000 gp --- The additional damage from the rune granted by the thurible is 2d6, and the spell DC is 35. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 6th-level _divine wrath._ **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_divine decree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495). Judgment Thurible (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Price** 65,000 gp --- The additional damage from the rune granted by the thurible is 2d6, and the spell DC is 43. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 7th-level _divine decree_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_summon deific herald_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1007).
name: Judgment Thurible (Greater)

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- A jyoti’s feather is a shimmering red and gold feather, seeming almost metallic, although delicate and flexible to the touch. Though most aren’t made from true jyoti feathers, as the reclusive outsiders avoid visitors from the Material Plane, their connection to positive energy lent the spellhearts their name.
  • **Armor** You gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331) and negative energy.
  • **Weapon** The weapon has the [_disrupting_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2852) rune while the feather is affixed.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689). Jyoti's Feather **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Price** 960 gp --- **Activate** command (emotion, enchantment, incapacitation); **Requirements** The target must be fascinated by the mirror; **Effect** You control the target for 30 days, with the effect of a critically failed saving throw against [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87). If the victim sees the mirror again at any point before this duration expires, the control extends for an additional 30 days from the moment it looked at the mirror again. While there is no initial saving throw, the DC to break free due to commands against the creature’s nature is 35. Jyoti's Feather (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Price** 4,100 gp --- The item bonus when affixed to armor is +3. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 5th-level _vital beacon_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_healing well_](/Spells.aspx?ID=926). Jyoti's Feather (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 126 **Price** 9,400 gp --- The item bonus when affixed to armor is +4, and the rune when affixed to a weapon is _greater disrupting_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 6th-level _healing well_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_field of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1526).
name: Jyoti's Feather

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- The first of these strips of glittering cloth was worn by a monk from Jinin who would interweave it into his handwraps of mighty blows. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 24.
  • **Armor** You gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against [illusions](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=6) and [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387).
  • **Weapon** ([visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) After you cast an illusion spell by activating the scarf, the weapon is shrouded in a mesmerizing illusory pattern. Your next Strike causes the target to be [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round if it hits. If you don’t make a Strike by the end of your next turn, the illusion ends with no effect.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_dancing lights_](/Spells.aspx?ID=58). Lightweave Scarf **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 500 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast _[_hypnotic pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1564)_. Lightweave Scarf (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 5,500 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 6th-level _hypnotic pattern_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_vibrant pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1741). Lightweave Scarf (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 22,000 gp --- Item bonus when affixed to armor is +3; when affixed to a weapon, creatures are [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute; and the spell DC is 38. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 8th-level _vibrant pattern_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_scintillating pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1980).
name: Lightweave Scarf

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- Any armor or weapon this glass prism is affixed to glows softly with shifting colored lights. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 35.
  • **Armor** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) After you cast an abjuration spell by activating the prism, you glow with [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) in a multitude of shimmering hues, shedding light like a torch and making you [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) until the end of your next turn.
  • **Weapon** After you cast an [abjuration](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=1) spell by activating the prism, your Strikes with the weapon gain the [_brilliant_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2833) property rune until the end of your next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585). Radiant Prism **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 8,600 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_prismatic armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=973). Radiant Prism (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 20,500 gp --- The concealment when affixed to armor lasts for 2 rounds, and the spell DC is 38. The damage from the _brilliant_ rune increases to 2d4 of each type. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast _prismatic armor._ **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_prismatic wall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=234). Radiant Prism (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 64,000 gp --- The concealment when affixed to armor lasts for 2 rounds, and the spell DC is 43. The rune is _greater brilliant_, and its damage is 3d4 of each type. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast _prismatic armor_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_prismatic sphere_](/Spells.aspx?ID=232).
name: Radiant Prism

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- This two-pronged fork of metal emits a constant low hum, vibrating slightly when touched. The spell attack roll of any spell cast by activating this item is +9, and the spell DC is 19.
  • **Armor** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 2 to sonic damage and a +1 item bonus to saving throws against effects with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) trait.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a sonic spell by activating the fork, the weapon reverberates with trapped sound waves. Your next Strike causes the target to be [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 round if it hits (or for 3 rounds on a critical hit). If you don’t make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) by the end of your next turn, the sound waves dissipate with no effect.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_bullhorn_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1971). Resonating Fork **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 140 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_biting words_](/Spells.aspx?ID=868). Resonating Fork (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 340 gp --- The [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) is 5 and the item bonus is +2 when affixed to armor, the deafened duration when affixed to a weapon is 3 rounds (1 minute on a critical success), the spell attack roll is +13, and the spell DC is 23. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 2nd-level _biting words_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_sound burst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616). Resonating Fork (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 1,800 gp --- The resistance is 10 and the item bonus is +2 when affixed to armor, the deafened duration when affixed to a weapon is 1 minute (1 hour on a critical success), and the spell DC is 29. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-level _sound burst_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_painful vibrations_](/Spells.aspx?ID=964).
name: Resonating Fork

summary_markdown: This two-pronged fork of metal emits a constant low hum, vibrating slightly when touched. The spell attack roll of any spell cast by activating this …

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- This two-pronged fork of metal emits a constant low hum, vibrating slightly when touched. The spell attack roll of any spell cast by activating this item is +9, and the spell DC is 19.
  • **Armor** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 2 to sonic damage and a +1 item bonus to saving throws against effects with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) trait.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a sonic spell by activating the fork, the weapon reverberates with trapped sound waves. Your next Strike causes the target to be [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 round if it hits (or for 3 rounds on a critical hit). If you don’t make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) by the end of your next turn, the sound waves dissipate with no effect.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_bullhorn_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1971). Resonating Fork **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 140 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_biting words_](/Spells.aspx?ID=868). Resonating Fork (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 340 gp --- The [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) is 5 and the item bonus is +2 when affixed to armor, the deafened duration when affixed to a weapon is 3 rounds (1 minute on a critical success), the spell attack roll is +13, and the spell DC is 23. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 2nd-level _biting words_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_sound burst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616). Resonating Fork (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 1,800 gp --- The resistance is 10 and the item bonus is +2 when affixed to armor, the deafened duration when affixed to a weapon is 1 minute (1 hour on a critical success), and the spell DC is 29. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-level _sound burst_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_painful vibrations_](/Spells.aspx?ID=964).
name: Resonating Fork

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- Slowly spinning at the center of this crystalline orb is a single snowflake, and its surface remains lightly covered in frost no matter how hot the weather is outside. The spell attack roll of any spell cast by activating this item is +7, and the spell DC is 17.
  • **Armor** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 2 to cold.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) spell by activating the crystal, your [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the weapon deal an additional 1d4 cold damage until the end of your next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_ray of frost_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539). Rime Crystal **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 55 gp Rime Crystal (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 425 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 3rd-level [_chilling spray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1975). Rime Crystal (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 2,800 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_ice storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2001). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_cone of cold_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559).
name: Rime Crystal

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- A pointed vampire fang hovers within this smoked-glass vial, its tip crimson with slowly dripping blood. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 25.
  • **Armor** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 3 to [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and a +2 item bonus on saving throws against effects with the negative trait.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a [necromancy](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=7) spell by activating the fang, your Strikes with the weapon deal 1d4 persistent bleed damage until the end of your next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_chill touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745). Sanguine Fang **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 650 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_vampiric touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736). Sanguine Fang (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 1,750 gp --- The resistance is 5 when affixed to armor, the persistent bleed damage is 1d6 when affixed to a weapon, and the spell DC is 29. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_sanguine mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=989). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-level _vampiric touch_. Sanguine Fang (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 5,750 gp --- The resistance is 10 and the item bonus is +3 when affixed to armor, the persistent bleed damage is 2d6 when affixed to a weapon, and the spell DC is 34. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 5th-level _vampiric touch_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_vampiric exsanguination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735).
name: Sanguine Fang

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- A pointed vampire fang hovers within this smoked-glass vial, its tip crimson with slowly dripping blood. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 25.
  • **Armor** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 3 to [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and a +2 item bonus on saving throws against effects with the negative trait.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a [necromancy](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=7) spell by activating the fang, your Strikes with the weapon deal 1d4 persistent bleed damage until the end of your next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_chill touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745). Sanguine Fang (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 1,750 gp --- The resistance is 5 when affixed to armor, the persistent bleed damage is 1d6 when affixed to a weapon, and the spell DC is 29. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_sanguine mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=989). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-level _vampiric touch_. Sanguine Fang (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 5,750 gp --- The resistance is 10 and the item bonus is +3 when affixed to armor, the persistent bleed damage is 2d6 when affixed to a weapon, and the spell DC is 34. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 5th-level _vampiric touch_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_vampiric exsanguination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735).

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- This jagged, bony growth narrows to a sharp, pointed tip. Deep groves from some former battle scar its surface. The bearer of a saurian spike often feels a sudden surge in power and confidence.
  • **Armor** You gain [imprecise ](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=2412) with a range of 30 feet.
  • **Weapon** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) When you cast a [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) spell by activating the spike, your battle form is exceptionally fearsome. Each enemy in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must succeed at a DC 27 Will saving throw or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure).
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_gouging claw_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546). Saurian Spike **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 700 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_dinosaur form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1489). Saurian Spike (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 1,400 gp --- The range of the scent and fear emanation are both 60 feet. The save DC is 28. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 5th-level _dinosaur form_. Saurian Spike (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 128 **Price** 6,500 gp --- The range of the scent and fear emanation are both 60 feet. The scent is precise instead of imprecise, and creatures that fail their saves are frightened 2 (frightened 3 on a critical failure) with a DC 34 save. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 7th-level _dinosaur form_.
name: Saurian Spike

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 129 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- Carved in this wooden disc is a humanoid eye, painted in muted shades that blend in with the wood. The eye’s pupil continually twitches and moves, scanning its bearer’s surroundings.
  • **Armor** ([revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=686)) You aren’t [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) creatures of the eye’s level or lower, or creatures of the eye’s level or lower using [surprise attack](/Rules.aspx?ID=2589), though you can still be [flanked](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375).
  • **Weapon** When you make a Strike with the weapon against a target that’s concealed, hidden, or undetected, the DC of your [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to target it is 4 if the target is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) or 10 if it’s hidden or undetected.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485). Vigilant Eye **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 129 **Price** 650 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_web of eyes_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1032). Vigilant Eye (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 129 **Price** 1,250 gp --- The [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) DC if the eye is affixed to a weapon is 3 if the target is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) or 9 if it’s [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_clairvoyance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466). Vigilant Eye (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 129 **Price** 2,700 gp --- The flat check DC if the eye is affixed to a weapon is 2 if the target is concealed or 8 if it’s hidden. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_clairvoyance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_prying eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661).
name: Vigilant Eye

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 129 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- This small figurine is carved from soapstone in the shape of a deity or guardian, facing both front and back to indicate unflagging vigilance. markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 129 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- The tip of a metallic dragon’s claw is set into a band of silver. When you summon dragons using the claw, you must choose [metallic dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=35).
  • **Armor** After you cast a [conjuration](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=2) spell by activating the wyrm claw, you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to acid, cold, electricity, and fire for the next minute.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a conjuration spell by activating the claw, you gain a Breath Weapon activity that takes 2 actions and deals 6d6 damage with a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. Choose the area and type of damage when you use Breath Weapon: a 30-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) that deals acid, electricity, or fire, or a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) that deals cold or fire. You can use this activity once before the end of your next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_gouging claw_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546). Wyrm Claw **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 129 **Price** 1,000 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_summon dragon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1697). Wyrm Claw (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 129 **Price** 6,500 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 10, and damage from Breath Weapon when affixed to a weapon is 9d6 (DC 34). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 7th-level _summon dragon_. Wyrm Claw (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 129 **Price** 40,000 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 15, and damage from Breath Weapon when affixed to a weapon is 12d6 (DC 41). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_summon draconic legion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1008).
name: Wyrm Claw

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 130 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- Iron strips line the body of an accursed staff, capping the bottom and folding into an intricate knot at the top. While wielding an _accursed staff_, you’re empowered by the curses you inflict. If an enemy fails a saving throw against a spell you cast that has the [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) trait, you gain [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to double that spell’s level. These temporary Hit Points last 10 minutes. The enemy must be a significant threat and can’t have been a willing subject of the curse. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Accursed Staff **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 130 **Price** 250 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_daze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482)
  • **1st** [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447), [_ill omen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566)
  • **2nd** [_blood vendetta_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1454), [_warrior’s regret_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1030)
Accursed Staff (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 130 **Price** 900 gp ---
  • **3rd** [_claim curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=884), [_cup of dust_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2347)
  • **4th** [_ outcast’s curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620), [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250), [_warrior’s regret_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1030)
Accursed Staff (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 130 **Price** 4,000 gp ---
  • **5th** [_inevitable disaster_](/Spells.aspx?ID=930), [_mariner’s curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1593)
  • **6th** [_blinding fury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1968), [_feeblemind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614), _remove curse_
name: Accursed Staff

While wielding an _accursed staff_, you’re empowered by the curses you inflict. If an enemy fails a saving throw against a spell you cast that has the [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) trait, you gain [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to double that spell’s level. These temporary Hit Points last 10 minutes. The enemy must be a significant threat and can’t have been a willing subject of the curse. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Accursed Staff **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 130 **Price** 250 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_daze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482)
  • **1st** [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447), [_ill omen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566)
  • **2nd** [_blood vendetta_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1454), [_warrior’s regret_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1030)
Accursed Staff (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 130 **Price** 900 gp ---
  • **3rd** [_claim curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=884), [_cup of dust_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2347)
  • **4th** [_ outcast’s curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620), [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250), [_warrior’s regret_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1030)
Accursed Staff (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 130 **Price** 4,000 gp ---
  • **5th** [_inevitable disaster_](/Spells.aspx?ID=930), [_mariner’s curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1593)
  • **6th** [_blinding fury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1968), [_feeblemind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614), _remove curse_
name: Accursed Staff (Major)

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 130 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- The visages of beasts are carved into the painted wood of a _beast staff_, with a large head on top. When used as a weapon, the staff is a _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)_. While wielding the staff while you have it prepared, you’re affected by [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680). If you have Wild Empathy, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus on checks using it. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Beast Staff **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 131 **Price** 340 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_gouging claw_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546)
  • **1st** [_magic fang_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1657), [_pest form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1626)
  • **2nd** [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1440), [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514)
Beast Staff (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 131 **Price** 1,400 gp --- Used as a weapon, the staff is a +2 striking staff. The circumstance bonus if you have Wild Empathy is +2.
  • **3rd** _animal form_, [_insect form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1575)
  • **4th** _animal form_, [_bestial curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1966), _insect form_, _pest form_
Beast Staff (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 131 **Price** 6,250 gp --- Used as a weapon, the staff is a _+2 greater striking staff_. The circumstance bonus if you have Wild Empathy is +2.
  • **5th** _animal form, insect form_, [_moon frenzy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1609)
  • **6th** [_baleful polymorph_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479), _moon frenzy_
name: Beast Staff

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 131 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- A _boreal staff_ is chiseled from a cylinder of ice to form a spiky, jagged icicle, its surface gleaming with the colors of the northern lights. It gives the air around you a distinct chill. When used as a weapon, a _boreal staff_ is a _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)_. **Activate** command; **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** Attempt a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) using the staff. That Strike deals 2d4 additional cold damage. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Boreal Staff **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 131 **Price** 500 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_ray of frost_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539)
  • **1st** [_chilling spray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1975), [_snowball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=540)
  • **2nd** _chilling spray_, _snowball_
  • **3rd** _chilling spray_, [_elemental absorption_](/Spells.aspx?ID=901) (water only)
Boreal Staff (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 131 **Price** 2,750 gp --- When used as a weapon, this is a _+2 striking [frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839) staff_.
  • **4th** _chilling spray_, [_ice storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2001)
  • **5th** [_ cone of cold_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559), [_mantle of the frozen heart_](/Spells.aspx?ID=946)
Boreal Staff (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 131 **Price** 13,500 gp --- When used as a weapon, this is a _+3 greater striking frost staff_. The additional cold damage from the activation ignores cold [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318).
  • **6th** _cone of cold_
  • **7th** _cone of cold_, [_frigid flurry_](/Spells.aspx?ID=916)
name: Boreal Staff
